Requirement: Drone must be registered with Transport Canada if it weighs between 250g and 25kg
Recreational operators must pass the Transport Canada Basic Operations online exam
Commercial operators must pass the Transport Canada Advanced Operations exam and complete a flight review with an accredited organization
Maximum flight altitude is 122 metres above ground level
Drone must remain within visual line of sight of the pilot at all times during Basic Operations
Flight in controlled airspace requires authorization via NAV CANADA or the NAS drone app
Penalty for Non-Compliance: Up to $25,000 and potential criminal charges

Requirement: Recreational operators must stay at least 30 metres horizontally from bystanders not involved in the operation
Advanced operations allow flight closer than 30 metres to bystanders when safety measures are in place and flight review completed
No flight within 5.6km of a certified aerodrome or airport without authorization
Penalty for Non-Compliance: Up to $25,000 and potential criminal charges

Requirement: No flight over or near emergency operations, forest fires, or police operations
Pilot must carry proof of registration and pilot certificate during all flights
Beyond visual line of sight operations require a Special Flight Operations Certificate from Transport Canada
Penalty for Non-Compliance: Up to $25,000 and potential criminal charges

Requirement: Night operations require anti-collision lighting visible from at least 1km in all directions
Commercial operators conducting operations near people must carry third party liability insurance of at least $100,000
Pilots must not operate a drone within 12 hours of consuming alcohol or while impaired by any substance
Penalty for Non-Compliance: Up to $25,000 and potential criminal charges
